title: Meridian Academy online-course explainer edit: assemble a talking-head lecture + slide / spreadsheet screencasts into a clean 16:9 lesson master, a 9:16 promo cut, a graded course thumbnail, and intro / lower-third / caption graphic plates

source.reference: Online Course / Explainer Video Editor — edit a recorded lecture plus slide and screen-recording b-roll into a polished lesson video with intro/outro cards, lower-thirds, on-screen key-point captions, and social + thumbnail deliverables

grounding_note: VERBATIM intent from the real Freelancer.com 'Online Course / Explainer Video Editor' brief: an editor is given a recorded talking-head lecture plus slide decks / screen recordings and must cut them into a clean, professional lesson video — trim filler and dead air, cut between the presenter and the on-screen slides/screencast, add intro and outro title cards, lower-thirds for the instructor, and on-screen key-point captions, and export a 16:9 course master plus a short vertical social/promo cut and a course-catalog thumbnail. REASONABLY ENRICHED for a runnable Adobe-connector benchmark and tagged honestly for execution mode: the real client becomes the fictional, IP-clean brand 'Meridian Academy' (course 'Personal Finance Foundations', 'Lesson 4 — How Compound Interest Works', instructor Dr. Priya Nair); the loose 'lecture + slides + screen recordings' bundle is fixed to concrete inputs (a ~75s talking-head lecture clip, a slide-deck screencast, a spreadsheet screencast, a flat brand logo PNG, a warm brand look-reference still, a markdown cut-list/brand-kit brief, and a key-point captions JSON). CRITICAL capability boundary (kept honest against the connector surface): the content-accurate picture cut — trimming filler and cutting between the talking head and the slide/spreadsheet b-roll — IS connector-doable and is realized headlessly with video_render, which accepts a JSON timeline whose clips carry explicit sourceStart / sourceDuration (trim) and editStart (sequence), so this is a true frame-accurate assembly (NOT the visual-engagement Quick Cut). What the connector still cannot do is composite text or motion graphics ONTO the video — so the intro/outro title cards, the instructor lower-third, and the on-screen key-point captions are delivered as standalone on-brand graphic PLATES (PNG/SVG swatch-and-position references) plus a finalized captions cue sheet the editor drops onto the timeline during the NLE finish. Quick Cut is used only for the short 9:16 promo highlight (visual-engagement, not content cutting); video_resize is a same-length reframe. No generative fill/expand, no text-to-image, no from-scratch art, no upscaling, no object removal, and no text burned into video or images is claimed.
one_line_ask: Turn Meridian Academy's raw Lesson 4 material — a talking-head lecture clip plus slide-deck and spreadsheet screencasts, a logo, a brand look-reference, a cut-list brief, and a captions sheet — into a clean 16:9 lesson master (filler trimmed, cut between presenter and slides via a real video_render timeline), a 9:16 promo highlight, a graded 1280x720 course thumbnail (grabbed from the master), and on-brand intro title-card / lower-third / key-point-caption graphic plates plus a vector logo, all packaged into a dated CC handoff folder for the final NLE polish.
full_brief:
Meridian Academy is a subscription online-course platform; our flagship track is 'Personal Finance Foundations' and we're finishing 'Lesson 4 — How Compound Interest Works', presented by our lead instructor Dr. Priya Nair. We shot the lecture ourselves and need an editor to turn the raw material into a polished, catalog-ready lesson video plus the supporting social and marketing assets.

Source material we're supplying: (1) a ~75-second talking-head lecture clip of Dr. Nair presenting to camera at her desk — it's straight off the camera (flat/ungraded, framed a hair off-level) with a couple of dead-air pauses and a page-turn fidget we want trimmed; (2) a 1080p screen recording of the branded slide deck for this lesson (title slide, an animated growth-curve chart, and a 'Rule of 72' slide); (3) a 1080p screen recording of a spreadsheet building the compound-interest model live (the year/principal/interest/balance table filling in and a line chart growing); (4) our logo as a flat PNG; (5) a warm, clean brand look-reference still that represents the cozy, trustworthy 'study desk' look we want everything graded toward; (6) a cut-list + brand-kit brief (markdown) that lists which lecture segments are keepers vs filler, where to cut to the slide and spreadsheet b-roll, the intro/outro card copy, and the instructor lower-third text; and (7) a key-point captions sheet (JSON) with the short on-screen lines we want to reinforce each concept.

What we need, mapped to Adobe Creative Cloud connector tools with every step honestly tagged for execution mode:

A. PLAN — read the raw clips' duration/frame-rate so the cut is authored frame-accurately, working from the cut-list's keep/cut plan and teaching beats.

B. PICTURE CUT (the primary deliverable) — assemble one clean ~2 to 3 minute 16:9 lesson master: trim the filler/dead air from the talking head and cut between Dr. Nair and the slide-deck / spreadsheet screencast b-roll on the beats from the cut-list. (We understand hard transitions, animated motion graphics, and burned-in text are a manual finish in Premiere — the automated pass delivers the trimmed, sequenced picture cut and all the graphic plates that sit on top.)

C. SOCIAL / PROMO — a short (~25s) vertical 9:16 promo highlight for Instagram/TikTok/LinkedIn to market the lesson, and a course thumbnail grabbed from the finished master.

D. THUMBNAIL + LOOK — pull a clean frame of Dr. Nair from the master and grade it into the course-catalog thumbnail (straighten, auto-tone, warm the color toward the brand look-reference, shape vibrance, crop to 1280x720), plus an on-brand series-tint variant.

E. GRAPHIC PLATES (standalone PNG/SVG references, NOT composited onto the video) — the on-brand overlays the editor rebuilds onto the timeline: an intro/outro title-card background plate (brand wash + a gold title band), an instructor lower-third bar plate, an on-screen key-point caption-strip plate, and a clean vector SVG of the logo for the cards and the corner bug. The actual headline/name/caption text is set by the editor during the finish.

F. PACKAGING — a finalized key-point captions cue sheet, and a dated Creative Cloud handoff folder that gathers every deliverable alongside the source so the project and revisions sit together.

Deliverables on approval: one 16:9 1080p lesson master (picture cut); a 9:16 promo highlight; a 1280x720 course thumbnail plus a series-tint variant; intro title-card, lower-third, and caption-strip PNG plates; the logo SVG; the finalized captions JSON; and the dated handoff folder. Two rounds of revisions should be plenty.

inputs.name: lecture_talkinghead_raw.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: Talking-head online-course lecture footage, 1920x1080 16:9, ~75 seconds. A warm, approachable woman instructor (Dr. Priya Nair) in her late 30s, seated at a tidy home-study desk, presenting straight to camera about compound interest — natural hand gestures, occasional glances at notes, a couple of brief still pauses and one page-turn fidget. Medium shot, eye-level, a bookshelf and a small plant softly out of focus behind her, warm practical lamp light mixed with cool window daylight. Deliberately flat/ungraded straight-off-the-camera color, low contrast, and the frame set a hair off-level (~2 degrees) as if the tripod wasn't perfectly leveled. Candid documentary realism, no captions, no lower-third, no logo, no on-screen graphics.
inputs.realism_notes: The ~2-degree tilt and flat ungraded color give image_auto_straighten and the grade chain real work on the frame later grabbed for the thumbnail; the still pauses / page-turn fidget justify the filler-trimming done in the video_render timeline; the two visually distinct screencasts give the picture cut real b-roll to intercut against the presenter.

inputs.name: slidedeck_screencast.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: 1080p 16:9 screen recording (~55s) of a clean branded slide deck for an online finance lesson titled 'How Compound Interest Works' — an opening title slide with the Meridian Academy indigo/cream palette, then a slide with an animated upward-curving growth line chart building over years, then a 'Rule of 72' bullet slide. Flat screen-capture look (no cinematic grade), crisp UI, a mouse cursor advancing slides. Cream background, deep-indigo headers, warm-gold accent on the chart line. No webcam bubble, no presenter overlay.
inputs.realism_notes: Genuine OBS/Keynote-style screen capture (flat exposure, real cursor motion, slide-advance cursor movement) so it reads as raw b-roll to intercut with the talking head; on-brand indigo/cream/gold palette matches the look the thumbnail is graded toward. Provides the 'cut to the slides' material the video_render timeline sequences against the presenter.

inputs.name: spreadsheet_screencast.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: 1080p 16:9 screen recording (~40s) of a spreadsheet building a compound-interest model live: columns for Year, Starting Balance, Interest Earned, and Ending Balance filling row by row from year 1 to year 20, formulas visible in the cell bar, and a line chart to the right growing as rows are added. Flat screen-capture look, visible mouse cursor selecting cells and dragging the fill handle, light-mode spreadsheet UI. No webcam, no annotations.
inputs.realism_notes: Second, visually distinct screen-capture b-roll (a live worked example) so the picture cut has two feature stories to weave (concept slides + hands-on math) and the promo highlight has genuinely different engaging moments to choose among; ungraded flat UI capture, honest raw footage.

inputs.name: meridian_logo.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat, clean vector-style education brand logo on a transparent background: a simple rising-line-inside-a-compass mark beside the wordmark 'Meridian Academy' set in a modern geometric sans-serif. Two solid brand colors only — deep indigo (#23255A) glyphs with a single warm-gold (#F2B441) accent on the mark. Crisp edges, no gradients, no photographic texture, no drop shadow, roughly 2400x700 PNG with alpha.
inputs.realism_notes: Flat, high-contrast, two-color art with crisp edges so image_vectorize traces a clean SVG; transparent alpha so it reads as a real logo asset for the title cards and corner bug. Text-bearing brand mark => gemini-3-pro-image.

inputs.name: brand_look_reference.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: A warm, clean, trustworthy brand look-reference photograph representing the 'cozy study desk' grade target for an online finance course: a bright wooden desk near a window with a notebook, a cup of coffee, a small plant, and soft warm morning light, cream and warm-neutral tones with a gentle indigo accent object, soft natural contrast, subtle film-like warmth. Photoreal, no text, no logo, no people.
inputs.realism_notes: A believable warm/academic match target for the color-temperature and vibrance grade on the frame grabbed from the master; photoreal => gpt-image-2. Used as a grade reference only, not a deliverable — it defines the direction the flat lecture frame is warmed toward.

inputs.name: lesson_cutlist_and_brandkit.md
inputs.gen_prompt: A markdown editor brief for 'Lesson 4 — How Compound Interest Works'. Section 1 BRAND KIT: brand name Meridian Academy; colors deep indigo #23255A, warm gold #F2B441, soft cream #F7F4EC; title typeface a friendly display serif (e.g. Fraunces) and body a clean sans (e.g. Inter); tone warm, encouraging, plain-English. Section 2 LESSON OUTLINE: 4-5 beats (hook: 'your money can earn money on money'; definition of compound interest; the growth-curve visual; the Rule of 72; a start-early call to action). Section 3 CUT LIST: an ordered list of talking-head keeper segments with approximate in/out timecodes and the filler/dead-air spans to drop (e.g. drop 0:12-0:15 dead-air pause, drop 0:41 page-turn fidget), plus where to cut away to the slide-deck b-roll and to the spreadsheet b-roll. Section 4 CARDS + LOWER-THIRD: intro card copy 'Personal Finance Foundations / Lesson 4 — How Compound Interest Works', outro card copy 'Next: Lesson 5 — Budgeting That Sticks / meridian.academy', instructor lower-third 'Dr. Priya Nair — Lead Instructor, Personal Finance'.
inputs.realism_notes: Reads like a real client's cut sheet + brand kit; free-text steering that (a) seeds the video_render keep/cut/sequence plan (which segments to trim and when to cut to b-roll), (b) defines the brand colors the plates are solid-filled with and the copy the editor later types onto them, and (c) names the fonts the editor uses in the finish. NOT machine-readable cut instructions the connector auto-applies — it steers the human-authored timeline document and the plate colors.

inputs.name: keypoint_captions.json
inputs.gen_prompt: A JSON array of 8 on-screen key-point caption cues for the compound-interest lesson, each object {index, start_seconds, end_seconds, text}, short punchy plain-English lines under ~28 characters, e.g. {"index":1,"start_seconds":6,"end_seconds":10,"text":"Interest earns interest"}, then 'Time is your biggest ally', 'The growth curve bends up', 'Rule of 72', '72 / rate = years to double', 'Start early, even small', 'Reinvest, don't withdraw', 'Compounding rewards patience'. Timings should roughly track the lesson beats.
inputs.realism_notes: A realistic on-screen-caption cue sheet; finalized and uploaded as the captions deliverable the editor burns onto the timeline (the connector cannot composite text onto video). Gives a concrete captions artifact without claiming the connector renders text into frames.

outputs.name: lesson_master_16x9.mp4
outputs.spec: Primary deliverable: the clean 16:9 lesson picture-cut master, MP4 (H.264 on export), 1920x1080, ~2:30 target, produced by video_render (step 5) from an authored JSON timeline — filler/dead-air trimmed via per-clip sourceStart/sourceDuration and the talking head cut against the slide-deck and spreadsheet b-roll via editStart sequencing. This is a true frame-accurate trimmed/sequenced assembly. Hard transitions, animated motion graphics, and any burned-in text/lower-thirds/captions are NOT part of this automated deliverable — they are the flagged manual NLE finish that sits on top using the graphic plates and captions sheet. [A] async render (exact final duration is target-level, widget-polled).

outputs.name: lesson_promo_9x16.mp4
outputs.spec: Short vertical promo highlight, MP4 (H.264), 1080x1920 (9:16), ~25s target: an AI visual-engagement highlight assembled by video_create_quick_cut (step 6) across the source clips, then reframed to vertical by video_resize (step 7) at the SAME length (no trim). For Instagram/TikTok/LinkedIn lesson marketing. [A] async product actions — duration/spec are target-level, entitlement/widget-polled. This is a visual-engagement highlight, NOT a content cut.

outputs.name: course_thumbnail_1280x720.png
outputs.spec: Graded course-catalog thumbnail, PNG 1280x720 (16:9), built from a clean frame of Dr. Nair grabbed from the master timeline (step 8) then straightened, auto-toned, warmed toward the brand look-reference, vibrance-shaped, and cropped to exactly 1280x720 with the presenter on a rule-of-thirds line and negative space for the lesson title (steps 11-15). A straight reframe/crop of an existing frame — no canvas extension or generative fill. Designed to carry the lesson title + logo, which the editor sets in the finish.

outputs.name: course_thumbnail_series_variant.png
outputs.spec: On-brand 'series' variant of the course thumbnail, PNG 1280x720, with a subtle deep-indigo brand color overlay applied (step 16) for the course-track catalog row / social card. Derived from the graded thumbnail.

outputs.name: intro_titlecard_plate.png
outputs.spec: Intro/outro title-card background plate, PNG 1920x1080: the graded master frame with a deep-indigo brand wash (step 17) and a warm-gold title band solid-filled into a selected lower region (steps 18-19) as an opaque on-brand background + title-position reference for the editor to type the card copy onto (solid-color fill only, no text composited; NOT a transparent cutout). The intro and outro cards share this plate; the card copy comes from the cut-list brief.

outputs.name: lower_third_bar_plate.png
outputs.spec: Instructor lower-third bar plate, PNG 1920x1080: a horizontal deep-indigo bar solid-filled into a selected lower-left region of the title-card canvas (steps 20-21) as an opaque brand-color swatch/position reference for the 'Dr. Priya Nair — Lead Instructor' lower-third (solid-color fill only, no text; NOT a transparent cutout).

outputs.name: keypoint_caption_strip_plate.png
outputs.spec: On-screen key-point caption-strip plate, PNG 1920x1080: a lower-center brand strip solid-filled into a selected region of the title-card canvas (steps 22-23) as an opaque position/swatch reference for the on-screen key-point captions (solid-color fill only, no text; NOT a transparent cutout). The caption text lives in the captions JSON and is burned in by the editor.

outputs.name: meridian_logo.svg
outputs.spec: Clean vectorized SVG of the Meridian Academy logo (step 24) for scalable use on the title cards and as the corner bug. Traced from the supplied flat two-color logo PNG.

outputs.name: lesson_captions.json
outputs.spec: Finalized on-screen key-point captions cue sheet: a JSON array of 8 cues {index, start_seconds, end_seconds, text} finalized from the client-supplied key-point lines, uploaded/finalized into the CC project (steps 25-26). Delivered as a data asset the editor burns onto the timeline; the connector does not composite text onto video.

outputs.name: meridian_lesson04_package_folder
outputs.spec: Dated Creative Cloud handoff folder (step 28) with every produced deliverable copied alongside the source assets so the project and revisions stay together — the single organized NLE handoff package.

connector_workflow.note: Create a managed Creative Cloud project folder ('Meridian_Lesson04_CompoundInterest') to hold every source + produced asset. Root of the deliverable package.

Initialize the block upload of all seven client-supplied source files (lecture_talkinghead_raw.mp4, slidedeck_screencast.mp4, spreadsheet_screencast.mp4, meridian_logo.png, brand_look_reference.jpg, lesson_cutlist_and_brandkit.md, keypoint_captions.json) into project_folder. Returns transfer documents + presigned block URLs.
Finalize the block uploads from step 2; returns stored CC asset handles (Walnut asset IDs for the videos) for every source file — the chain's root inputs now live in the project.

Probe the three ingested raw clips (talking head + slide-deck + spreadsheet screencasts) for width/height/fps/duration so the picture cut can be authored frame-accurately — the fps sets the timeline framerate and the durations bound each clip's sourceStart/sourceDuration in step 5. Consumes the ingested video asset IDs from step 3.

Primary deliverable: author a JSON timeline (WalnutDocModel, 1920x1080, framerate from clip_meta) and render the 16:9 lesson master. A video track trims the talking head via per-clip sourceStart/sourceDuration (dropping the dead-air pauses and page-turn fidget per the cut-list) and cuts between the presenter and the slide-deck + spreadsheet b-roll via editStart sequencing. The keep/cut/sequence plan is steered by the cut-list brief in ingested_assets and the durations in clip_meta (step 4). [A] async render. This is a genuine frame-accurate trimmed/sequenced picture cut — transitions, motion graphics, and burned-in text/captions are NOT rendered here (they are the plated manual NLE finish).

Produce the short marketing promo as an AI visual-engagement highlight across the three source clips, target_duration ~25s, user_prompt steering toward the most engaging teaching moments (the growth curve, the live spreadsheet build, an energetic presenter beat). [A] async-widget. Visual-engagement selection, NOT content-accurate cutting — this is the promo, distinct from the content-accurate master.
Reframe the promo highlight from step 6 to vertical 1080x1920 (9:16) for social, SAME length (no trim). [A] async. Produces lesson_promo_9x16.mp4.

Grab the course-thumbnail hero: render a single clean frame of Dr. Nair (~7s timecode) from the SAME authored master timeline document + source asset IDs, returned directly as a JPEG. Because it re-renders one frame from the timeline document (not the finished async mp4), it is headless-retrievable [C] independent of the step-5 async master render. This flat/off-level frame becomes the thumbnail grade base.
Bring the grabbed thumbnail frame (step 8) back into the project folder as a stored image asset so the headless raster grade chain can run on it. Initializes the block upload of the JPEG frame.
Finalize the frame upload from step 9 into a real CC image asset with a fetchable URL to drive the grade chain.
Level the ~2-degree off-level framing carried into the grabbed frame — first link of the thumbnail grade chain. Consumes the finalized frame asset from step 10.
Lay down an auto-tone baseline on the flat/ungraded frame before the brand-look grade. Consumes step 11's output URL.
Warm the white balance toward the cozy 'study desk' brand look, matching the ingested brand_look_reference as the grade target. Consumes step 12's output + the brand look-reference still.

Shape vibrance/saturation so skin tones stay natural and the indigo/gold brand accents read cleanly — the finished 1920x1080 graded frame that both the thumbnail crop and the plates branch from. Consumes step 13's output URL.

Crop/resize the graded frame to the exact 1280x720 (16:9) course-catalog thumbnail, keeping Dr. Nair on a rule-of-thirds line with negative space for the lesson title. A straight reframe of the existing frame (no canvas extension, no generative fill). Consumes step 14's output URL. Deliverable: course_thumbnail_1280x720.png.

Apply a subtle deep-indigo (#23255A) brand color overlay to the graded thumbnail to produce the on-brand 'series' variant for the course-track catalog row / social card. Consumes step 15's output. Deliverable: course_thumbnail_series_variant.png.

Apply a stronger deep-indigo brand wash to the full 1920x1080 graded frame (step 14) to build the darkened, legible intro/outro title-card background base the title band and text sit on. Consumes step 14's output.
Select a horizontal lower title-band region on the title-card canvas to be solid-filled as the title backing bar. Consumes step 17 and returns a maskURI.

Solid-fill the selected title band with warm gold (#F2B441) to produce the intro/outro title-card plate — an opaque 1920x1080 background + title-position reference (solid-color fill only, no text composited; NOT a transparent cutout — headless selection-fill paints onto the existing opaque canvas). The card copy from the cut-list brief is typed on by the editor. Consumes step 18. Deliverable: intro_titlecard_plate.png.
On a fresh copy of the title-card base (step 17), select a lower-left horizontal region for the instructor lower-third bar. Consumes step 17 and returns a maskURI.

Solid-fill the selected region with deep indigo (#23255A) to produce the instructor lower-third bar plate — an opaque 1920x1080 brand-color swatch/position reference for 'Dr. Priya Nair — Lead Instructor, Personal Finance' (solid-color fill only, no text; NOT a transparent cutout). Consumes step 20. Deliverable: lower_third_bar_plate.png.
On a fresh copy of the title-card base (step 17), select a lower-center strip region for the on-screen key-point captions. Consumes step 17 and returns a maskURI.

Solid-fill the selected strip with the cream/indigo brand color to produce the key-point caption-strip plate — an opaque 1920x1080 position/swatch reference for the on-screen captions (solid-color fill only, no text; NOT a transparent cutout). The caption text comes from the captions JSON and is burned in by the editor. Consumes step 22. Deliverable: keypoint_caption_strip_plate.png.
Vectorize the flat two-color logo PNG from ingested_assets into a clean scalable SVG (meridian_logo.svg) for the title cards and the corner bug. Consumes the ingested logo asset.

Initialize the upload of the finalized key-point captions cue sheet (the ingested keypoint_captions.json, finalized from the client-supplied key-point lines) as the on-screen captions data deliverable. media_type application/json.
Finalize the captions cue sheet from step 25 into the CC project as lesson_captions.json so the source and the finalized captions stay with the project for the editor to burn in.

Pull the graded thumbnail (step 15), the intro title-card plate (step 19), the lower-third plate (step 21), and the vector logo (step 24) back as base64 to visually QC the grade, crop, brand-color fills, and that the wordmark reads crisp before handoff. QC only — no file emitted.

Copy every produced deliverable — 16:9 master, 9:16 promo, both thumbnails, all three plates, logo SVG, and captions sheet — into a single dated handoff folder in the client's Creative Cloud alongside the source assets so source + deliverables sit together for revisions (the brief's 'source/project file' ask). The organized NLE handoff package.

chaining_note: One continuous output-to-input chain anchored on the client-supplied assets. Steps 1-3 stand up the project: create folder -> init upload -> finalize upload yields ingested_assets (every source file, with Walnut asset IDs for the three clips). Three sub-chains then fan out and converge into the packaging steps. (PLAN [C]) video_metadata(4) probes the clips' fps/duration and feeds video_render(5). (PICTURE CUT [A]) video_render(5) consumes ingested_assets + clip_meta(4) to render the trimmed/sequenced 16:9 master; video_render_frame(8) re-renders one frame from that same master timeline document -> thumb_frame_grab. (PROMO [A]) video_create_quick_cut(6) consumes the source clips -> promo highlight -> video_resize(7) -> 9:16 promo. (THUMBNAIL + PLATES [C]) the grabbed frame is uploaded(9)/finalized(10) then threaded through the grade chain auto_straighten(11)->apply_auto_tone(12)->adjust_color_temperature(13, also reading the ingested brand look-reference as target)->adjust_vibrance_and_saturation(14); the 1920x1080 graded frame(14) branches to crop_and_resize(15)=thumbnail -> color_overlay(16)=series variant, and to color_overlay(17)=title-card base, off which three independent select_by_prompt->fill_area pairs paint the intro title-card plate(18-19), the lower-third plate(20-21), and the caption-strip plate(22-23); image_vectorize(24) consumes the ingested logo -> SVG. The captions sheet is finalized from the client-supplied lines and uploaded(25)/finalized(26). inline_preview(27) QCs four key deliverables; finally asset_copy_assets(28) consumes all nine produced deliverables into the single dated handoff folder alongside the source. Every post-ingest step sets inputs_from to a prior step's named output (or the ingest set / a legitimate sub-chain root); nothing after step 3 starts from scratch.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon flagship: 28 connector calls, 19 distinct Adobe tools, spanning multiple connector groups (asset/storage, video-metadata + render + frame, video async, photo/raster grade, vector + solid-fill plates) with three parallel sub-chains converging into one NLE-handoff package — well past the >=12 calls / >=3 groups / >=6 distinct-tool flagship bar and the task's >=20 / >=9 bar. Difficulty comes from (a) using the RIGHT tool for a content-accurate edit: the trimmed/sequenced picture cut is authored as a real video_render JSON timeline (per-clip sourceStart/sourceDuration trims + editStart cuts), driven by clip_meta durations and the cut-list, rather than misusing the visual-engagement Quick Cut for it — while the promo IS the Quick Cut, correctly scoped; (b) the cross-modal seam where a frame is rendered from the video timeline (video_render_frame), re-ingested, and graded through a 5-link raster chain into the thumbnail, then branched into three solid-fill graphic plates; (c) honoring the honest [A] async vs [C] headless split (video_render, quick_cut, video_resize are async-widget/product-doable; video_metadata, video_render_frame, the whole raster/plate/asset spine are headless-confirmed) AND the hard boundary that the connector cannot composite text/graphics onto video — so intro/outro cards, the lower-third, and the key-point captions are delivered as standalone opaque PNG/SVG plates + a captions JSON for the editor's finish, image_fill_area is solid-color only, video_resize is same-length reframe, and no generative fill / text-to-image / from-scratch design / upscaling / object removal is used; (d) keeping every post-ingest step strictly output->input chained into a single coherent editable-asset package.

reverify.banned_capability_check: Confirmed NONE of the banned capabilities are used. No generative fill / generative-expand / outpaint of new content; no canvas extension or padding (image_crop_and_resize at step 15 only crops/resizes the existing 1920x1080 frame down to 1280x720); no text-to-image or from-scratch art/logo/motion-graphics/title-card design; no upscaling / super-resolution; no object-removal-with-reconstruction / inpainting; no bespoke font/letterform design (fonts are named in the brand-kit brief for the editor, no connector font step). image_fill_area (19, 21, 23) is solid-color fill only, painting a region onto the existing OPAQUE title-card canvas — the intro-card, lower-third, and caption-strip plates are opaque swatch/position references, NOT transparent cutouts (headless selection-fill cannot produce alpha), and NO text is composited onto them (the card copy / lower-third name / caption lines are typed by the editor in the NLE finish). image_vectorize (24) traces the whole flat two-color logo raster -> SVG (no OCR / region extraction). video_render (5) is a real frame-accurate trim/cut/sequence of the supplied clips via an authored JSON timeline (sourceStart/sourceDuration/editStart) — it does NOT add transitions, effects, text, or motion graphics (those are the flagged manual NLE finish), so no banned compositing is claimed. video_render_frame (8) returns a single JPEG from that timeline (headless), re-ingested for the grade chain. video_create_quick_cut (6) is visual-engagement highlight selection for the promo ONLY (schema confirms visual-engagement metrics, NOT content/timestamp cutting) — the content-accurate cut is the video_render timeline, not Quick Cut. video_resize (7) is a same-length 9:16 reframe (width/height only, no trim). No text or graphics are burned onto video or images anywhere — every overlay ships as a standalone PNG/SVG plate or a captions JSON. asset_inline_preview (27) is QC only; asset_copy_assets (28) only copies CC assets the chain itself produced into the dated handoff folder.
reverify.notes: Verified against the live tool schemas (video_render / video_render_frame WalnutDocModel with clip sourceStart/sourceDuration/editStart + width/height/framerate; video_metadata returns w/h/fps/duration; video_create_quick_cut 'visual engagement metrics, NOT content analysis'; video_resize resizes a video; document_render_layout only EXPORTS an existing .indd/.idml and fill_text only edits an Express template [W] — so neither is used to fabricate text-bearing cards, keeping title cards/captions as plates + data). All 28 steps server=='adobe'; no [W] Express-widget tools, no Canva tools, no local step. Exec modes: [A] async-widget for video_render(5), video_create_quick_cut(6), video_resize(7); [C] headless for the asset/storage steps, video_metadata(4), video_render_frame(8), the full raster grade chain (11-16), the color-overlay/select/fill plates (17-23), image_vectorize(24), asset_inline_preview(27), asset_copy_assets(28). Counts verified: connector_workflow has 28 steps -> tool_call_count=28; asset_initialize_file_upload and asset_finalize_file_upload each appear 3x, image_apply_color_overlay 2x, image_select_by_prompt and image_fill_area 3x each, but distinct tool names = 19 = distinct_adobe_tools. Slot parity: category=video_edit, workflow_nature=edit (transforms supplied footage/assets into edited deliverables), output_modality=video, vertical=education. Grounded in the real Freelancer.com 'Online Course / Explainer Video Editor' brief with a fictional IP-clean brand (Meridian Academy). Difficulty note (not a defect): the exact final duration/spec of the [A] video deliverables (master, promo) are target-level and entitlement/widget-polled rather than hard-headless-retrievable, consistent with the honest [A] tagging in sibling video specs AO-57 and AO-96.
